items.name: 勅使門
items.subcategory: 扁額「勅使山」

en.description_en: Imperial Envoy's Gate (Chokushimon) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it bears a framed tablet reading 'Chokushizan.' It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1974. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara 1864, Kamisato.

items.name: 十二天古画
items.subcategory: 紙本対幅。一幅に六体描かれる。

en.name_en: Old Painting of the Twelve Devas
en.description_en: Old Painting of the Twelve Devas is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a pair of hanging scrolls on paper, each depicting six figures. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

items.name: 阿弥陀三尊古画
items.subcategory: 絹本着色。阿弥陀三尊来迎図

en.name_en: Old Painting of the Amida Triad
en.description_en: Old Painting of the Amida Triad is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a color-on-silk painting depicting the descent of the Amida triad. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

items.name: 天神古画
items.subcategory: 絹本着色。天神端座之図

en.description_en: Old Painting of Tenjin is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a color-on-silk painting of Tenjin seated upright. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 594-1, Kamisato.

items.name: 伝武田信玄・陽雲院夫妻画像
items.subcategory: 寛文9年(1669)狩野元俊筆。陽雲院の服装に葡萄模様が配されている。

en.name_romaji: Den Takeda Shingen, Yōun'in Fūsai Gazō
en.name_en: Portraits Said to Be of Takeda Shingen and His Wife Yōun'in
en.description_en: Portraits Said to Be of Takeda Shingen and His Wife Yōun'in is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, they were painted by Kanō Mototoshi in 1669 (Kanbun 9), and Yōun'in's robe is patterned with grapevine motifs. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1974. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

items.name: 十五仏古画
items.subcategory: 絹本着色。中央に虚空蔵菩薩が描かれる。

en.name_en: Old Painting of the Fifteen Buddhas
en.description_en: Old Painting of the Fifteen Buddhas is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a color-on-silk painting with Kokūzō Bosatsu depicted at the center. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 594-1, Kamisato.

items.name: 不動明王古画
items.subcategory: 脇侍はコンガラ・セイタカ童子が描かれる。

en.description_en: Old Painting of Fudō Myōō is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, the attendants Kongara and Seitaka Dōji are depicted at its sides. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Oshibo 1469, Kamisato.

items.name: 古銅正観音立像
items.subcategory: 新羅三郎義光懐中守本尊、陽雲寺遺品と伝えられる。

en.name_en: Old Bronze Standing Statue of Shō-Kannon
en.description_en: Old Bronze Standing Statue of Shō-Kannon is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to be the personal guardian icon of Shinra Saburō Yoshimitsu and a relic of Yōun-ji. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

items.name: 釈迦如来像
items.subcategory: 陽雲院遺品。

en.description_en: Statue of Shaka Nyorai is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to be a relic of Yōun'in. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

items.name: 正観音立像
items.subcategory: 金箔玉眼嵌入寄木造り。寺伝では恵心僧都作と伝えられている。

en.description_en: Standing Statue of Shō-Kannon is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a gilt joined-wood statue with inlaid crystal eyes, held by temple tradition to be the work of the priest Eshin Sōzu. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

items.name: 阿弥陀如来座像
items.subcategory: 金箔玉眼嵌入。町内最大の仏像

en.description_en: Seated Statue of Amida Nyorai is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a gilt statue with inlaid crystal eyes, recorded as the largest Buddhist statue in the town. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Ōmidō 1151, Kamisato.

items.name: 金銅釈迦如来像
items.subcategory: 宝暦13年(1736)癸未9月17日鋳工江戸神田多川民部の銘がある。

en.description_en: Gilt-Bronze Statue of Shaka Nyorai is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it bears an inscription dated the 17th day of the 9th month of Hōreki 13 (given as 1736) by the caster Tagawa Minbu of Kanda, Edo. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 3316-1, Kamisato.

items.name: 薬師座像
items.subcategory: 慶長10年（1605）の墨書銘がある。

en.description_en: Seated Statue of Yakushi is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it bears an ink inscription dated Keichō 10 (1605). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Tsutsumi 474, Kamisato.

items.name: 勝軍地蔵
items.subcategory: 木造漆箔朱色玉眼嵌入

en.description_en: Shōgun Jizō (Victory Jizō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a wooden statue with lacquer gilding, vermilion coloring, and inlaid crystal eyes. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Jinbohara-machi 263, Kamisato.

items.name: 銅鐘
items.subcategory: 元禄八身乙亥十月廿三日(1695)佐野治工井上元峰作

en.description_en: Bronze Bell is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it was made by the Sano metal caster Inoue Genpō on the 23rd day of the 10th month of Genroku 8 (1695). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

items.name: 三条実美公寄贈野剣
items.subcategory: 銘周防国永弘。高橋泥舟の添書がある。

en.name_en: Sword Donated by Lord Sanjō Sanetomi
en.description_en: Sword Donated by Lord Sanjō Sanetomi is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is signed 'Nagahiro of Suō Province' and is accompanied by a note by Takahashi Deishū. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

items.name: 徳川家康皆済状
items.subcategory: 慶長11年(1606)代官長谷川長次宛

en.name_en: Certificate of Full Payment Issued by Tokugawa Ieyasu
en.description_en: Certificate of Full Payment Issued by Tokugawa Ieyasu is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is dated Keichō 11 (1606) and addressed to the magistrate Hasegawa Nagatsugu. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on October 29, 1987. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara 1369, Kamisato.

items.name: 薬師写経高泉禅師筆
items.subcategory: 紺地金泥薬師如来写経

en.name_en: Yakushi Sutra Transcription Brushed by Zen Master Kōsen
en.description_en: Yakushi Sutra Transcription Brushed by Zen Master Kōsen is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a transcription of the Yakushi Nyorai sutra in gold ink on a dark blue ground. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Nagahama 1774, Kamisato.

items.name: 陽雲寺中世文書
items.subcategory: 武田家ゆかりの中世文書7点

en.description_en: Medieval Documents of Yōun-ji Temple is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, they are seven medieval documents connected with the Takeda family. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 27, 1974. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

items.name: 文禄四年水帳
items.subcategory: 文禄4年（1595）武州賀美郡鉢形筋金窪之内黛村

en.description_en: Land Register of Bunroku 4 is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it covers Mayuzumi village within Kanakubo, Hachigata district, Kami county, Bushū Province, dated Bunroku 4 (1595). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Mayuzumi 86, Kamisato.

items.name: 天海僧正御墨付
items.subcategory: 寛永19年(1642)3月28日東叡山直末状

en.description_en: Written Authorization by Archbishop Tenkai is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a Tōeizan direct-branch temple certificate dated the 28th day of the 3rd month of Kan'ei 19 (1642). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Mayuzumi, Kamisato.

items.name: 親子地蔵
items.subcategory: 表裏に地蔵の陰刻の有る画像板石卒塔婆勅使河原直重親子の墓と伝えられる。

en.description_en: Parent-and-Child Jizō is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is an incised stone stupa tablet bearing images of Jizō on both faces, said to be the grave of Teshigawara Naoshige and his child. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara 1864, Kamisato.

items.name: 埴輪頭部
items.subcategory: 神流川と烏川の合流地より出土したと伝えられる。

en.description_en: Head of a Haniwa Figure is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to have been excavated at the confluence of the Kanna and Karasu rivers. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1974. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

items.name: 布目瓦
items.subcategory: 五明廃寺出土瓦。軒平瓦3種・軒丸瓦3種

en.description_en: Cloth-Patterned Roof Tiles is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, they are tiles excavated from the Gomyō temple ruins: three types of flat eave-tiles and three types of round eave-tiles. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Gomyō 761, Kamisato.

items.name: 石棒
items.subcategory: 石神社御神体

en.description_en: Stone Rod (Sekibō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is the sacred object of Ishi Shrine. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Jinbohara-machi 1384, Kamisato.

items.name: 石棒
items.subcategory: 御神体として祭られていたと伝えられる

en.description_en: Stone Rod (Sekibō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to have been enshrined as a sacred object. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 1950, Kamisato.

items.name: 浅間山古墳
items.subcategory: 角閃石安山岩を使用した横穴式石室

en.description_en: Sengen'yama Tumulus is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it has a horizontal stone chamber built of hornblende andesite. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Jinbohara-machi 15, Kamisato.

Stone Slab Stupa is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is inscribed with the date Kōchō 4 (1264). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 594-1, Kamisato.

items.name: 寺浦1号古墳出土埴輪群
items.subcategory: 大字長浜の寺浦1号古墳から出土した埴輪群。 男女の人物埴輪4点、馬形埴輪1点の計5点で構成される

en.name_en: Haniwa Group Excavated from Teraura Tumulus No. 1
en.description_en: Haniwa Group Excavated from Teraura Tumulus No. 1 is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a group of five haniwa excavated from Teraura Tumulus No. 1 in Ōaza Nagahama, comprising four male and female human figures and one horse figure. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on December 22, 2021. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

See-Through Stone Lantern is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it was erected in Bunka 12 (1815). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara 1864, Kamisato.

items.name: 石幢
items.subcategory: 六面地蔵石幢。永正6年(1509)銘

en.description_en: Stone Pillar Lantern (Sekidō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a six-sided Jizō stone pillar inscribed with the date Eishō 6 (1509). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara 1864, Kamisato.

items.name: 赤羽刀
items.subcategory: 戦中に接収された刀・槍・薙刀6点

en.description_en: Akabane Swords is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, they are six swords, spears and glaives confiscated during the war. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on September 15, 2001. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

items.name: 忍保神楽
items.subcategory: 池上神社に伝わる神楽

en.description_en: Oshibo Kagura (Sacred Dance) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a kagura sacred dance handed down at Ikegami Shrine. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Oshibo, Kamisato.

items.name: 黛音頭
items.subcategory: 樽・鼓・鐘・笛に音頭取りと舞方による音頭

en.description_en: Mayuzumi Ondo (Folk Song and Dance) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is an ondo performed with barrel drum, hand drum, bell and flute, together with a lead singer and dancers. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Mayuzumi, Kamisato.

items.name: 諏訪神社獅子舞
items.subcategory: 貞享2年(1685)銘の獅子頭。

en.description_en: Lion Dance of Suwa Shrine is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, its lion-head mask is inscribed with the date Jōkyō 2 (1685). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Sanchō, Kamisato.

Lion Dance of Nyū Shrine is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara, Kamisato.

Lion Dance of Kanakubo Shrine is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo, Kamisato.

Lion Dance of Shichihongi Shrine is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on November 3, 1976. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi, Kamisato.

items.name: 東音頭
items.subcategory: 長幡地区を発祥とする音頭

en.description_en: Azuma Ondo (Folk Song and Dance) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is an ondo originating in the Nagahata district. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: the Nagahata district, Kamisato.

items.name: 乾武神流川太鼓
items.subcategory: 平成3年に活動を開始した創作和太鼓。海外公演など、町内外で文化的活躍を続ける

en.description_en: Kanbu Kanna River Drums is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a creative Japanese drum group active since 1991 (Heisei 3), performing within and beyond the town, including overseas. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on December 22, 2021. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi, Kamisato.

items.name: 陽雲院之墓
items.subcategory: 武田信玄内室陽雲院の墓

en.description_en: Grave of Yōun'in is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is the grave of Yōun'in, a consort of Takeda Shingen. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

items.name: 帯刀先生義賢之墓
items.subcategory: 源義賢の墓

en.description_en: Grave of Tatewaki-senjō Yoshikata is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is the grave of Minamoto no Yoshikata. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Tatewaki 309-2, Kamisato.

items.name: 五輪塔
items.subcategory: 逆修僧都円宗。天文15年(1546)

en.description_en: Five-Ring Stupa (Gorintō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a pre-death memorial for the priest Enshū, dated Tenbun 15 (1546). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Fujikido 29-1, Kamisato.

items.name: 神流川古戦場
items.subcategory: 天正10年(1582)神流川合戦が行われた古戦場。

en.name_en: Old Battlefield of the Kanna River
en.description_en: Old Battlefield of the Kanna River is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is the site of the Battle of the Kanna River, fought in Tenshō 10 (1582). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Bishato, Kamisato.

items.name: 金窪館跡
items.subcategory: 治承年間(1177~1180)加治家季築城と伝えられる。
items.location: 上里町大字金久保1570　他

en.description_en: Site of Kanakubo Manor is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to have been built by Kaji Ietoshi during the Jishō era (1177-1180). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 1570 and elsewhere, Kamisato.

Memorial Shrine for Hata Tokiyoshi is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

items.name: 柿の大木
items.subcategory: 字柿ノ木発祥の木

en.description_en: Great Persimmon Tree is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, the place name Kakinoki is said to originate from this tree. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1974. Recorded location: Jinbohara-machi 425, Kamisato.

items.name: ひいらぎの大木
items.subcategory: 樹齢450年

en.description_en: Great Holly-Olive Tree is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, the tree is about 450 years old. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1974. Recorded location: Ōaza Tsutsumi 519, Kamisato.

items.name: マキの木
items.subcategory: 樹齢800年

en.description_en: Podocarpus (Maki) Tree is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, the tree is about 800 years old. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Ōmidō 737, Kamisato.
Pine Tree is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1974. Recorded location: Ōaza Oshibo 1632, Kamisato.

Yōjigyo (a Small Freshwater Fish) is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: the Oshibo River, Kamisato.
